column2: A newly installed SOHO router still uses its factory administrator credentials. Which security setting should be changed?
column4: Change the default administrator password to a strong password.
column5: Default router credentials are easy to obtain, and administrator access allows configuration changes. This protects the management account; it does not by itself control which clients may join the network.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.10; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 46
column10: A+::220-1202::2.10 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::SecuringaSOHONetwork Basic HighYield RouterSecurity Credentials Management Source::Messer-v140

column2: A SOHO administrator must block traffic associated with one specific IP address while leaving other addresses allowed. Which router setting matches the requirement?
column4: An IP filtering deny rule for that address.
column5: IP filtering makes allow-or-deny decisions using addresses or address ranges. Content filtering is the nearby alternative when the requirement concerns a URL or category within the traffic instead.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.10; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 46
column10: A+::220-1202::2.10 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::SecuringaSOHONetwork Basic IPFiltering AccessControl Scenario Source::Messer-v140

column2: A router manufacturer releases firmware that corrects a security flaw. What should the administrator do to apply that protection?
column4: Update the router to the current supported firmware.
column5: SOHO appliances commonly receive fixes through manufacturer firmware. Keeping firmware current applies security patches as well as other supported fixes and improvements.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.10; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 46
column10: A+::220-1202::2.10 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::SecuringaSOHONetwork Basic HighYield Firmware PatchManagement RouterSecurity Source::Messer-v140

column2: A small office wants its router to block websites in an inappropriate-content category. Which setting should it configure?
column5: Content filtering can act on URLs or website categories. IP filtering instead fits a requirement stated in terms of specific network addresses or ranges.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.10; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 46
column10: A+::220-1202::2.10 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::SecuringaSOHONetwork Basic ContentFiltering WebSecurity Scenario Source::Messer-v140

column2: A router is mounted where visitors can reach its cables and reset controls. What physical security change should the administrator make?
column4: Move or secure the router in a location accessible only to authorized people.
column5: Physical access can bypass or disrupt logical settings. Placement should also preserve useful wireless coverage and allow authorized maintenance, so it should be planned rather than merely made inconvenient.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.10; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 46
column10: A+::220-1202::2.10 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::SecuringaSOHONetwork Basic PhysicalSecurity NetworkEquipment Placement Source::Messer-v140

column2: Applications on a SOHO network are automatically opening inbound router ports without administrator approval. Which feature should be disabled unless a required application needs it?
column5: UPnP supports zero-configuration discovery and lets internal applications request inbound openings. Disabling it restores deliberate administrator control over that exposure.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.10; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 46
column10: A+::220-1202::2.10 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::SecuringaSOHONetwork Basic HighYield UPnP PortSecurity RouterSecurity Source::Messer-v140

column2: A small office must host a public service while adding separation between that service and its internal systems. Where should it place the public resource?
column5: A screened subnet is positioned between the Internet and the internal network for public-facing resources. It is the current term for what was commonly called a DMZ in this network design.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.10; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 46
column10: A+::220-1202::2.10 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::SecuringaSOHONetwork Basic HighYield ScreenedSubnet Segmentation PublicServices Source::Messer-v140

column2: A SOHO router needs administration only from inside the local network. Which management-access change best matches that requirement?
column4: Disable remote management and allow management only from the local network.
column5: Limiting where administrative sessions can originate reduces exposure of the management interface. Strong credentials and an additional factor, if available, protect the account itself and complement the location restriction.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.10; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 46
column10: A+::220-1202::2.10 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::SecuringaSOHONetwork Basic HighYield ManagementAccess RemoteAccess RouterSecurity Source::Messer-v140

column2: A new access point broadcasts a factory SSID that reveals its brand. What wireless setting should the administrator change?
column4: Replace the default SSID with a less obvious network name.
column5: The service set identifier is the wireless network name. Changing an obvious default avoids advertising a factory name, but the new name still needs real authentication and encryption protections.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.10; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 46
column10: A+::220-1202::2.10 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::SecuringaSOHONetwork Basic SSID WirelessSecurity Configuration Source::Messer-v140

column2: An administrator disables SSID broadcast. What security result should the administrator expect?
column4: The network name disappears from ordinary discovery lists, but wireless analysis can still identify it.
column5: SSID suppression provides obscurity rather than strong access control. It does not replace WPA2 or WPA3 encryption and authentication.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.10; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 46
column10: A+::220-1202::2.10 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::SecuringaSOHONetwork Basic HighYield SSID WirelessSecurity Discovery Source::Messer-v140

column2: A SOHO wireless network uses one shared credential and must not remain open. Which encryption configuration should the administrator choose?
column4: WPA2-Personal or WPA3-Personal with a pre-shared key.
column5: Personal mode fits a shared-key SOHO requirement. Enterprise mode instead authenticates users individually through an authentication service and is a different deployment model.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.10; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, pp. 46-47
column10: A+::220-1202::2.10 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::SecuringaSOHONetwork Basic HighYield WPA2 WPA3 Encryption WirelessSecurity Source::Messer-v140

column2: A SOHO router has guest wireless access enabled by default, but the business does not provide guest access. What should the administrator do?
column5: An unneeded guest network creates unnecessary access. If guest access is actually required, it should be deliberately configured and protected with WPA2 or WPA3 rather than left open.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.10; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 47
column10: A+::220-1202::2.10 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::SecuringaSOHONetwork Basic HighYield GuestNetwork WPA2 WPA3 WirelessSecurity Source::Messer-v140

column2: Several active network jacks in a public conference room are not needed. What should the administrator do with their switch ports?
column5: An enabled jack is a connection point even when no approved device currently uses it. Disabling the associated port removes that access until an administrator deliberately enables it again.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.10; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 47
column10: A+::220-1202::2.10 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::SecuringaSOHONetwork Basic HighYield PortSecurity WiredSecurity PhysicalPorts Source::Messer-v140

column2: An internal service must accept connections sent to a specific port on the SOHO router's public address. Which firewall setting provides that mapping?
column5: The rule persistently maps an external IP address and port to an internal IP address and port. The two port numbers can differ, so the required mapping should be stated and configured deliberately.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.10; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 47
column10: A+::220-1202::2.10 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::SecuringaSOHONetwork Basic HighYield PortForwarding Firewall NAT PublicServices Source::Messer-v140
